content.text: Build and all 32 tests pass. Quick check that the class survived into the bundled CSS:

input.description: Confirm rainbow CSS is in the built stylesheet

content.text: The rainbow cursor is implemented. Since we're on the trail branch, I'll commit the change there.

input.command:
git add src/components/Board.jsx src/index.css && git commit -m "Make the falling piece render as an animated rainbow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>"
input.description: Commit rainbow cursor change on trail branch

result.output:
[make-the-cursor-a-rainbow a010070] Make the falling piece render as an animated rainbow
 2 files changed, 30 insertions(+), 3 deletions(-)

content.text:
Done — the `make-the-cursor-a-rainbow` trail is implemented and committed on its branch (`a010070`).

What changed:
- **`src/components/Board.jsx`** — active-piece cells are now tagged `active: true` when overlaid on the board, and `cellClass` renders them with a `rainbow-cell` class instead of the piece's solid color. Ghost outlines and locked pieces keep their normal per-piece colors.
- **`src/index.css`** — added the `.rainbow-cell` style: a six-stop rainbow gradient that continuously animates via background-position shift plus a full 360° hue-rotate on a 2-second loop, so the falling piece visibly cycles through the rainbow.

Verified: all 32 tests pass, `vite build` succeeds, and the `rainbow-cell` styles are present in the bundled CSS.

The commit is local only — say the word if you want me to push the branch so the trail can be merged.
